fact: The accident occurred at Montparnasse station in Paris in 1895 (specifically October 22, 1895)
The company fined drivers for delays, incentivizing the driver to come in too fast
source: Wikipedia — Montparnasse derailment (section on causes); widely documented in French railway histories

fact: The train's Westinghouse air brake failed to stop the locomotive in time
source: Wikipedia — Montparnasse derailment; contemporary reports in Le Petit Journal (October 1895)

fact: The train crashed through the buffer stop, crossed the concourse, and broke through the terminal wall, ending up on the street below
source: Wikipedia — Montparnasse derailment; the iconic Lévy & Fils photograph documenting the locomotive hanging from the façade

fact: One woman on the sidewalk below was killed by falling masonry
source: Wikipedia — Montparnasse derailment (the victim was Marie-Augustine Aguilard, a newspaper vendor on the Place de Rennes struck by debris)
